# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductSearchController < ApplicationController
  def index
    query = Product.arel_table
    params[:search].each do |key, value|
      case key
      when 'name'
        query = query.where(query[::name].matches("%#{value}%"))
      when 'category'
        category = Category.find_by(name: value)
        query = query.joins(:category).where(query[:categories].id => category.try(:id)) unless category.nil?
      when 'min_price', 'max_price'
        case key
        when 'min_price'
          query = query.where(price: { greater_than_or_equal_to: value.to_f })
        when 'max_price'
          query = query.where(price: { less_than_or_equal_to: value.to_f })
        end
      end
    end

    @products = query.result(distinct: true)
  end
end
